question: Did the model output the correct final piece sequence (2, 1, 3, 4) corresponding to the Top-Left, Top-Right, Bottom-Left, and Bottom-Right positions?
Did the model correctly identify that Image 1 and Image 2 require a 180-degree rotation (they appear upside down)?
Did the model correctly identify that Image 3 requires a 90-degree clockwise rotation (the table and drinks are sideways)?
Did the model correctly identify that Image 4 requires a 180-degree rotation (the rug and people appear upside down)?
Does the reasoning identify the 'OLYMPUS LOUNGE' neon sign as a key visual feature connecting Piece 2 (containing 'OLY...') and Piece 1 (containing '...MPUS LOUNGE')?

Does the reasoning mention the vertical continuity of the characters' bodies (e.g., the man in the striped suit) or wall elements to justify joining the top row (Pieces 2, 1) with the bottom row (Pieces 3, 4)?
Did the model identify the copper/bronze pipes or ceiling elements as visual continuity cues across the top pieces (Images 1 and 2)?
Did the model recognize the ornate burgundy and gold floor/rug pattern as a connecting feature for the bottom pieces (Images 3 and 4)?
If the model generated intermediate images of the pieces, are they displayed in the correct upright orientation (i.e., people standing vertically, text readable horizontally)?
If the model generated an image joining Piece 2 and Piece 1, is the text 'OLYMPUS LOUNGE' visually continuous and aligned across the seam?
If the model generated an image joining the top and bottom rows, are the standing figures (e.g., the man in the striped suit) aligned vertically without disjointed waists or mismatched legs?
If intermediate assembly images were generated, are the edges between joined pieces visually aligned without unnatural gaps or distortions?
Do the generated images preserve the original visual content of the pieces without hallucinating new objects or textures not present in the input?
If a final reconstructed image was generated, does it show a coherent single-room scene with ceiling elements on top and floor/rug on bottom, matching the semantic structure of a lounge?

If the text describes placing a specific piece (e.g., 'Piece 2 is top-left') or applying a rotation, does the corresponding generated image visually depict that specific placement or rotation accurately?
If the model claims two pieces share a matching edge feature (e.g., 'the pipes continue between pieces'), is this visually verifiable in the generated image?
Is the response free from contradictions between the text description and the visual content of generated images (e.g., text says 'A is above B' but image shows otherwise)?
